transcript: Hi. What is the postal code?
What is the postal code to, um, where you’re trying to ship this item to?
Whoever I was talking to before wanted to ship bananas to Switzerland and I need to know the postal code of where in Switzerland it’s going.
Hey, hello? Sorry about that. My phone is super messed up. Hey, Arthur! Hello, Arthur?
Hey, it’s Vito. What’re you doin’?
Oh yeah—h—hold on one sec. [sfx] Hey, sorry about that. Uh, hey, where can I get a newspaper around here? I’m new to the area here.
Okay, where’re they located? Here, hold on one sec. [sfx] Sorry about it. Are they within walking distance, or…?
I think I live right in the same street as you; that’s what my friend said. Uh, where’s Publix, anyhow?
Well what street are you on now?
[sfx] Uh, excuse me. Hey, maybe I could borrow your newspaper for a while? I’ll give it back. [sfx]
Okay, so then you can give me a ride maybe in the next hour or two, or…? [sfx]
[sfx] I don’t have a car. I have a bicycle.
Ride your bike. They’re all over the place.
W—I’d be happy to! I don’t know where the heck they’re located. I don’t know everything there is to know! I’m new here! [sfx]
Well, don’t know what else to tell ya.

Here, my brother’s here; he can maybe give ya some more information. I don’t even know this intersection where I am. Here, just talk Joe. My brother is Joe. Here. Hey, uh, hey, Joe? He’s on the phone right here, our neighbor.
Oh, my god! Are you fuckin’ asshole?
Can you tell me your name—what that is—who it is?
Get off my phone! You piece of shit! You’re gonna be in jail tonight ya motherfucker! You piece of Shit! Shit! Shit! You’re gonna drop dead tonight! Tonight, you motherfuckerr!
Hello! This is Jarvis callin’. I just moved in, ah, ‘round the corner here. How you doin’, there?
You just moved in around the corner here?
Yeah. Yeah, I got your number from Crazy Face on the other side of the street; that’s what we all call him. [chuckles] [delay]
Uh, no, I don’t know who you are.

Oh, yeah! I just live two houses down. My name’s Jarvis Buzzer and I got your number from, ah, Crazy Face down the street; he said, “Give ‘em a call. He’s a nice guy.” I’m just sayin’ hey! How you doin’ up ‘ere? [exhales] [delay]
I’m doin’ okay! Ah, gee, I don’t know who you are.
Oh, I’m real new. I’m brand spankin’ new up here!

Uh-huh. I got a new phone, I got a new house, and I got all this nubuck. I just wanted ta show it off a little bit. I’m gonna just pop by your place and just tell me what ya think. You into nubuck at all, or…? [delay]
Oh, it’s kinda like suede but, oh, it’s so much better! It’s tougher! And it’s real sharp! Real flippin’ shārp! Whadda ya think?
And, ah, Crazy Face gave me your number.
Glen! His last name is Alfred.
Oh! Glen! Glen! Glen! Glen! Oh! Y—they live next door to me, right?—
Yeah, yeah yeah! He said to give you a call—you’re a real nice guy—and just say, “Hi” and, ah, tell ya about my nubuck!
D’you mind if I put him on the phone? Crazy Face, here? And just say—
You can say, “Hey”—hey, hold on a second. Hey, pick up—
Again, you’re the same guy that called me today?
I didn’t call you today, did I? I don’t think—
What are you—why are you calling me? Can you tell me why you’re calling me?
N—I don’t know. Who are you? I didn’t—

You’re full of shit! You’re a piece of shit! Aren’t you—aren’t you—aren’t you worried about if God is gonna put you to death? Because you ha—bothering me? Don’t you think so, you sonofabitch? No, no, no, listen, [unclear, perhaps “Justin…”].
Don’t call back. I don’t know who you are. Somebody’s yellin’ and cussin’.
Hey, Bob! This is Sloan Babcock calling.

Hey, just real quick, I wanted to take a moment, honor our Four Sacred Directions with ya about seven o’clock tonight. “Upward, Downward, Leftward, Rightward”? At the fairgrounds. So can you participate in that tonight? That’d be wonderful! [laughs] So! [delay] [exhales]
Wow, man! This is tripped out!
Ya know, just to honor the Elders. So that’s why I wanted to—
I don’t have any idea who this is.
It mainly consists of braiding hair and pounding on the ground. [exhales] [whispers] So yeah! [delay] [exhales]
Hey, Bob, I just wanted to take a moment and honor our Four Sacred Directions with ye!
[restrained laughter] Just takes about 45 minutes o’ your time here tonight, here—
I know one young Elder who has some Bolivian nasal spray.
Wow! Sound like some fresh fish flake!
There’s some Special Atmospherics out there.
We can send an Uber by. Does that work?
Ah, ah, can I just drive myself?
Alright, well shoot me an address.
What can I do for ya?
I’m just callin’ everybody in the neighborhood today. We wanted to take a moment this evening to honor our Four Sacred Directions with ye: Upward, Downward, Leftward, and Rightward. And we s—
Well we send an Uber vehicle by about 7 PM tonight. And it takes about 30, 45 minutes of your time, is all.
Uh, I’m gonna forego that; tonight I have previous plans, so thank you so much, Merlin!
Well if you don’t wanna participate, we just ask for a $75 donation to cover the other Ubers that we’ll be sending.
O—only because I’m not really sure who you are—

...and if this is legitimate and, uh, I don’t even know you, Merlin, to be quite honest with you. And you’re asking me to jump in a Uber and go somewhere, and then when I decide not to do that you wanna try ta request some kind of a remittance. Mm, no, uh, I’m a little uncomfortable with all this, Merlin, so I’m just gonna hang up on you, okay?—
I’m Merlin Mumford. I’m an old—
Raccoon Hunter. I think we just got off on the wrong foot; I apologize about that.
I just have to—a—I just have to say, ah—
Did I not make myself clear?
My question was, “Did I not make myself clear?”
Uh, okay, I’m gonna block you now. I don’t see why this—
This event is very important to the community tonight, that’s all!
Okay, well I’m—I’m gonna let you go now. I’m—I’m gonna let ya—
No—no! You listen! Look—look, you know, you—a—you sound fairly threatening to me and, ah, I’ve asked you—
[stammers] I’m—I’m the one that makes that decision! I feel threatened by you. So stop calling me, okay?—
Can I just tell you what we do?—
Hey, I’m an old Raccoon Hunter, okay? That’s who I am. And tonight we’re gonna braid hair and pound on the ground! That’s what we’re gonna do!—
Great! We—good! God bless you! Bye-bye!
So get involved, guy. We’ll pick ya up about 7:30 or make a donation; it’s your call.
You’re being recorded. I just wanna let you know. You’re being recorded. Just stay on the line; that’s all I need you to do, is stay on the line.
Okay, just stay on the line—
My name is Merlin Mumford and my phone number is, uh, 979-0180.
That’s not the number that, ah, that I just received. But just keep talking! I just need you to keep talking.
Pueblo Police dispatch. This is Lindsay. How can I help you?

Yes, uh, I’m getting phone calls from some spurious individual. I don’t know who he is, but he calls himself “Merlin”. And, ah, he says he’s a neighbor but I’m not sure, exactly, who he is. But it seemed a little odd I—I—I have no idea why this phone went to you. Who a—

Well first, he wanted to know if I wanted to take an Uber drive someplace, you know, to venerate something-or-other. And then, ah, when I told him I had other plans he solicited me for a $75 donation. Uh, you know, I don’t know who this guy is, okay?
Okay—yeah! I mean, you can just block the number.
Okay, I’ll block the number! Thank you so much! I’m sorry to—
I—I—I have no idea why it went to you, but, ah—
I’m so glad it did because that guy was badgerin’ me. Like, I don’t know who he was.
Yeah, I would just block the number. It sounds like a scam.
You contacted the Lahina, Maui police station.
Yeah, well, I’m nowhere near Maui police station. I don’t know how this happened, but, uh, no, I’m n—I’m—I’m nowhere near Maui, so, I mean… I’m gonna hang up now, ‘kay?
